raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint regarding American Express failure to properly investigate and resolve my billing dispute case XXXX involving a purchase from XXXX for XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX tickets. 
XXXX sold me tickets for the XXXX  XXXX XXXX XXXX at a time when XXXX had not released any official tickets for public sale at the time. The official XXXX website clearly stated that tickets would only be made available through a lottery / application process and that no direct ticket sales were authorized at the time of my purchase. Therefore, the tickets advertised and sold by XXXX could not have been legitimate inventory and the transaction was inherently deceptive and based on misrepresentation. 

Despite submitting evidence to American Express proving that tickets were not officially available at the time of sale, American Express improperly processed my dispute as a " delivery timing issue '' rather than addressing the core issue of deceptive sales and misrepresentation XXXX! American Express accepted the merchants generic claim that tickets would be delivered closer to the event date without evaluating whether these tickets existed at the time of purchase. i have contacted American Express more than XXXX times through phones and online chats. At the first 4 times they refused to even open a case for me ; after i finally opened the case ; they easily closed my case and said the merchant advised the tickets will be sent closer to the event date which was completely not the reason why i tried to dispute this charge. i asked to reopen the case and Amex giving me a hard ; i tried XXXX more time they finally reopened for me but still treating this as a delivery timing issue, when my case is actually misrepresentation and sale of non-existent tickets. 
American Express failed to properly investigate my dispute involving misrepresentation and deceptive sale of XXXX XXXX XXXX tickets. Instead of addressing the fact that XXXX sold tickets that were not released and did not exist at the time of sale, Amex incorrectly processed the dispute as a delivery timing issue. This constitutes failure to conduct a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). The merchant response does not refute my evidence that tickets were unavailable at the time of purchase. 
American Express has therefore allowed a deceptive and unauthorized transaction to stand, causing me financial harm and undermining the protections afforded to consumers under federal law.
